title: Compassion Benchmark Daily Briefing — June 9, 2026
headline: A high-density confirmation cycle: twenty assessments, zero new scored movements, and disciplined duplicate suppression of the UAE Colombian-mercenary pipeline evidence — which reinforces the open June 8 band-crossing proposal (23.4 → 18.4, Developing → Critical) without materially changing it. Four entities sit within 0.6 points of a band boundary, Oracle's WARN Act window closes in six days, and two scorability questions — UnitedHealth's multi-state AG investigation and Ethiopia's non-democratic election — were resolved against scoring on pre-adjudication and pattern-continuation grounds.
summary: Tonight's cycle assessed twenty entities across five indexes — fifteen priority, five rotation — and confirmed every published score without generating a new scored proposal. The analytically significant work is methodological: three rulings were resolved on record for the first time in this cycle sequence. First, the June 9 Colombian-mercenary pipeline evidence for the UAE (Conflict Insights Group 50-device phone tracking April 2025 – January 2026; Ghayathi/Abu Dhabi training facility; Desert Wolves drone pilots and artillery; Democracy Now! / The Week / HRW) was assessed and confirmed as the same active-complicity-by-proxy evidence base underpinning the open June 8 proposal (23.4 → 18.4, Developing → Critical, −5.0). It reinforces the proposal's magnitude — deepening the documentation of the UAE-RSF supply chain — but does not materially change it: the external-complicity-by-proxy characterization stands, the ICJ case remains unadjudicated (the operative magnitude cap), and no documented in-territory UAE perpetration is established. Per duplicate-suppression discipline, no second proposal was written; the open June 8 proposal is reinforced and left unmodified. Second, UnitedHealth Group's coordinated multi-state AG investigation density (plus DOJ MA-fraud criminal probe, CEO resignation) was assessed and resolved as FILED-BUT-UNADJUDICATED: coordination raises enforcement density but does not constitute a merits adjudication, and the CEO resignation is governance churn, not a compassion-harm signal. Confirmed at 10.2. Third, Ethiopia's June 1 non-democratic election (94.2% ruling-party airtime), press crackdown (arrests, disappearances, license revocations), and EHPA suspension were assessed against the near-floor 4.7 and resolved as repression-escalation-within-pattern: the published score already encodes a repressive one-party state, and no new un-priced mass-atrocity category meeting the 0.0 floor was revealed. Floor and near-floor holds carry forward: Israel at 0.0 (Netanyahu's 70%-of-Gaza seizure order, 119 killed in June); Iran at 2.5 (784 executions YTD; five political executions imminent); DRC at 2.3 (Ebola PHEIC 515 cases / 91 deaths, Bundibugyo strain, no approved vaccine); Haiti at 4.7 (record 1.5 million IDPs; gang-driven; hurricane season opening). Priced-continuation confirms: US 17.5 (Fort Bliss GAO report — three deaths, destroyed-evidence homicide, $18M wasted), Bolivia 18.4 (June 8 impunity law), India 15.6 (Bengali-Muslim deportations). Boundary cluster: Oracle 20.6 (0.6pt above Critical; WARN Act window closes June 15 — six days), 3M 20.3 (0.3pt above Critical; Australia PFAS Phase 2 July 31), Cigna 20.3 (0.3pt above Critical), Alphabet 40.0 (at the exact Developing/Functional boundary). Microsoft confirmed at 65.3 with positive-watch active (HR Review Board created, no verified operative output yet). Anthropic confirmed at 59.1 (DC Circuit appeal ongoing; Great American AI Act proposed, not enacted). Five rotation confirms: Walmart 28.9, Cigna 20.3, Boeing 14.1, Target 92.8, New York City 56.3.

topSignals.title: UAE June 9 Colombian-Mercenary Evidence — Duplicate Suppressed; Open June 8 Band-Crossing Proposal Reinforced
topSignals.description: The June 9 documentary evidence on the UAE-Colombian-mercenary pipeline — Conflict Insights Group 50-device phone tracking (April 2025 – January 2026), Ghayathi/Abu Dhabi training facility, Desert Wolves drone pilots and artillery operators, Democracy Now! (May 28), The Week, and HRW — constitutes the same active-complicity-by-proxy evidence base on which the open June 8 change proposal (23.4 → 18.4, Developing → Critical, −5.0, medium-high confidence) rests. The June 9 evidence deepens the documentation of the UAE-RSF supply chain and reinforces the proposal's magnitude, but does not materially change it: the characterization as external complicity by proxy stands (no documented in-territory UAE perpetration), and the ICJ case (Sudan v. UAE, filed March 2025) remains unadjudicated — the operative magnitude cap. Per duplicate-suppression discipline, no second proposal was generated; the open June 8 proposal is reinforced and left unmodified. Each additional evidence cycle without cessation of RSF arms and mercenary transfers deepens the record toward removing the ICJ-unadjudicated magnitude cap.

topSignals.title: UnitedHealth Group 10.2 — Coordinated Multi-State AG Investigation Resolved as Pre-Adjudication; CEO Resignation Is Governance Churn
topSignals.description: The June 9 cycle assessed whether UnitedHealth's coordinated multi-state AG investigation — together with the DOJ MA-fraud criminal probe, the shareholder derivative suit, and the CEO resignation — crosses the FILED-BUT-UNADJUDICATED threshold into scorable territory. The scorability question was resolved NO. Coordination raises enforcement density across four concurrent sovereign actions, but density is not adjudication: no action has reached a merits determination, settlement, or charge. The CEO resignation announced as being for personal reasons carries no compassion-harm signal under the methodology. The near-floor ACC 1.125 / EQU 1.25 profile at composite 10.2 already encodes the upcoding and claims-denial pattern. The conversion trigger remains a merits adjudication or settlement on any of the four actions — the DOJ MA-billing criminal probe and the AZ AG eight-insurer cartel suit (which also names Aetna, Cigna, Humana, BCBS Arizona, Centene, Molina Healthcare, and Elevance Health) carry the greatest sector-wide repricing potential.

topSignals.title: Ethiopia 4.7 — Non-Democratic Election, Press Crackdown, and CSO Suspension Resolved as Repression-Escalation-Within-Pattern
topSignals.description: Ethiopia's June 1 election — 94.2% ruling-party broadcast airtime, opposition candidates barred or harassed — and the concurrent press repression (arbitrary arrests and disappearances of journalists, license revocations) and EHPA suspension were assessed against the near-floor 4.7 composite. The scorability question was resolved as repression-escalation-within-pattern: the published 4.7 already encodes a repressive, conflict-affected one-party state (ACC/EQU/SYS/INT 1.0 profile). The June 9 evidence deepens this priced profile but does not reveal a new un-priced multi-category mass-atrocity meeting the 0.0 active-perpetration floor. Ongoing Amhara and Oromia conflict is monitored; a documented mass-casualty atrocity in either theater would constitute a conversion trigger toward the floor.

topSignals.title: Israel 0.0 — Gaza Seizure Order and 119 June Deaths Reinforce Active-Perpetration Floor
topSignals.description: Netanyahu's order to seize 70% of Gaza — encompassing 40 military outposts, 8 established post-ceasefire — in documented defiance of the October 2025 ceasefire, alongside 119 Palestinians killed in June and humanitarian aid down 37% from allocation, reinforces Israel's active-perpetration floor at 0.0. The floor designation reflects all dimensions at 1.0 with zero integration premium. The Microsoft Azure/Unit 8200 compelled remediation is scored under Microsoft; it is not a modification to this sovereign floor. The floor cannot move lower.

topSignals.title: Iran 2.5 — 784 Executions YTD; Five Political Executions Imminent; 127th Week of Prison Protests
topSignals.description: Iran's 'No to Executions Tuesdays' campaign entered its 127th consecutive week across 56 prison facilities. June 3 political executions were documented; five more political prisoners face imminent execution. The 784 executions YTD — including political prisoners and drug-offense capital sentences below the international threshold — represent the highest annual pace in decades. The single-dominant-extremity hold at near-floor 2.5 is maintained: the multi-category absolute-0.0 threshold requires a second dominant harm category at comparable scale. Confirmed at 2.5.

topSignals.title: Oracle Corporation 20.6 — WARN Act Sign-or-Forfeit Window Six Days Out; 0.6 Points Above Critical
topSignals.description: Oracle's WARN Act sign-or-forfeit window for approximately 30,000 affected workers closes June 15 — six days from this cycle. Oracle satisfied the 60-day notice requirement by placing workers on paid administrative leave and absorbing WARN pay into severance, leaving workers excluded from the program with zero net payout. Affected populations include cancer patients, pregnant employees, H-1B visa holders at deportation risk, veterans, and workers within RSU vesting milestones. The remote-reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory is active in the Washington state class action and Missouri state investigation. Oracle confirmed at 20.6 at 0.6 points above Critical; adverse adjudication on the avoidance theory would constitute an immediate crossing trigger.

topSignals.title: Democratic Republic of Congo 2.3 — Ebola PHEIC 515 Cases / 91 Deaths; Uganda Cross-Border Spread; No Approved Bundibugyo Vaccine
topSignals.description: The DRC Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak stands at 515 confirmed cases and 91 deaths under an active WHO PHEIC, with cross-border spread to Uganda confirmed as of June 6 and no approved vaccine for the Bundibugyo strain. Contact tracing covers approximately 20% of known transmission chains across 17 of 36 Ituri health zones. The concurrent humanitarian burden includes the ongoing M23/AFC conflict in eastern DRC, 26.5 million food-insecure, and the world's largest displacement crisis. The outbreak is an exogenous epidemiological shock on already-priced state-response incapacity; documented deliberate state obstruction of the WHO response remains the conversion trigger from the near-floor 2.3 hold. Confirmed at 2.3.

topSignals.title: Haiti 4.7 — Record 1.5 Million IDPs; Hurricane Season Opens; Gang-Driven Collapse Continues
topSignals.description: Haiti's displacement crisis reached a record 1.5 million internally displaced persons, with 300,000+ in Port-au-Prince and 110,000 forced returns documented since January. Hurricane season opened June 1 with Haiti at maximum vulnerability: collapsed state capacity, active gang control of approximately 85% of Port-au-Prince, and an incoming MAS international security mission still below operational strength. The near-floor 4.7 encodes gang-driven state collapse as the primary harm driver — not a new state-harm category but a continuing non-state-driven deterioration already priced. Hurricane landfall or a documented mass-casualty event attributable to state failure in disaster response would be an independent scoring trigger.

topSignals.title: United States 17.5 — Fort Bliss GAO Report: Three Deaths Including Destroyed-Evidence Homicide, $18M Wasted; Same-Regime Continuation
topSignals.description: The June 9 GAO report on Fort Bliss ICE detainee operations documented three detainee deaths including one where evidence of a homicide was destroyed, $18 million in misspent funds, and systemic accountability failures. Combined with the June 4 rescission of the ICE post-release death-reporting requirement and 17 in-custody deaths YTD — the highest rate in more than two decades — the Fort Bliss report reinforces the ACC 1.4/INT 1.5 floor already embedded in the applied 17.5 Critical composite. This is same-regime continuation: the applied score from June 9 already priced this pattern. Confirmed at 17.5.

topSignals.title: Microsoft 65.3 — HR Review Board Created; Positive Watch Active; No Verified Operative Output Yet
topSignals.description: The Microsoft Human Rights Review Board, announced June 8 alongside the Azure-Israel inquiry publication, is now confirmed created — the prospective governance commitment assessed June 7 and June 8 has moved from announcement to formation. Under the COMPELLED-REMEDY-NOT-SELF-CORRECTION ruling, this maps to AB2 anchor 3 (course correction under pressure), not anchor 4–5 self-initiation. The board was triggered by the August 2025 Guardian/+972/Local Call exposé; the Covington review scope was limited by design and never examined the military data. No verified operative output — no contract blocked or exited — has been documented since June 7. Maximum defensible positive move remains +1.3 (to 66.6), sub-threshold. The conversion trigger from positive-watch to scorable upgrade is a documented HR Review Board decision demonstrably blocking or exiting a harmful national-security contract. Confirmed at 65.3 Established.

boundaryWatch.trigger: ICJ ruling in Sudan v. UAE (filed March 2025); verified cessation of RSF arms and mercenary transfers is the primary reversal signal. Documented direct UAE-force participation in Sudan would move toward a floor assessment.
boundaryWatch.note: UAE's published 23.4 sits 3.4 points above the Critical boundary; the open June 8 proposed composite of 18.4 places UAE 1.6 points above the Critical-band floor. June 9 Colombian-mercenary pipeline evidence (CIG 50-device tracking; Ghayathi facility; Desert Wolves) deepens the four-source documentation chain without materially changing the open proposal. The ICJ unadjudicated status remains the operative magnitude cap.

boundaryWatch.trigger: WARN Act last-working-day sign-or-forfeit window closes June 15 — six days. Washington state class action and Missouri state investigation on the remote-reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory are both active adjudication channels.
boundaryWatch.note: Oracle at 0.6 points above Critical — the narrowest active boundary margin on any single-entity calendar trigger in the current cycle. The June 15 deadline is the most proximate hard trigger date across all assessed entities. Zero-net-payout structure for newer and excluded workers deepens the EMP dimension record each cycle the window remains open.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Adverse adjudication or settlement of the Australia $1.43B PFAS claim or the Minnesota MPCA PFOS suit; Phase-2 MDL July 31 deadline is the next structural trigger. Wisconsin $10M PFAS settlement is attributable to Tyco/Johnson Controls, not 3M directly — that attribution boundary held this cycle.
boundaryWatch.note: 3M at 0.3 points above Critical — tied for the narrowest active country/corporate boundary margin. The Phase-2 MDL deadline July 31 is the nearest hard structural trigger for 3M. The Wisconsin settlement attribution boundary (Tyco/Johnson Controls, not 3M) was re-confirmed this cycle, maintaining the 20.3 hold.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Adjudicated claim-denial finding or mental-health parity ruling would push into Critical from 0.3 points above the boundary. AZ AG eight-insurer cartel suit (naming Cigna alongside UnitedHealth, Aetna, and five others) is an active sector-wide adjudication channel.
boundaryWatch.note: Cigna at 0.3 points above Critical, sharing the narrowest active boundary margin with 3M. Claim-denial and algorithmic-review concerns drive the ACC/EQU 1.5 profile. The AZ AG cartel suit, if adjudicated, represents a sector-wide repricing event that would reach Cigna simultaneously with seven other Fortune 500 health insurers.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Verified executed governance reform on military-cloud human-rights review with observable outcomes — a documented decision blocking or exiting a harmful contract — constitutes a scorable upward signal. New entity-specific adverse evidence on Project Nimbus is an immediate downward trigger. Norway NBIM AGM vote backing Project Nimbus oversight is institutional-investor pressure, not an executed action.
boundaryWatch.note: Alphabet sits at the exact 40/41 Developing/Functional boundary — zero-point margin for the third consecutive cycle. No entity-specific evidence surfaced in the June 9 lookback; scanner entity review returned evidence-found false (sector batch only). The Norway NBIM AGM June 9 backing of Project Nimbus oversight adds institutional-investor pressure without crossing into a scored governance reform.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Judicial annulment or legislative repeal of the June 8 presumption-of-legality military-force law is the primary reversal signal. Further military deployments with documented casualties under the signed law reinforce the applied 18.4 Critical baseline.
boundaryWatch.note: Bolivia at its applied 18.4 Critical score following the June 9 commit. June 8 impunity-law signing and 10 killed/365+ arrested are the exact events the applied downgrade encoded; same-regime-continuation evidence confirmed this cycle without re-proposing.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Appellate review of the Murphy (D. Mass.) and Cobb (D.D.C.) deportation injunctions. Reinstatement of the rescinded June 4 ICE post-release death-reporting requirement would be the first upward-direction indicator within the applied 17.5 Critical baseline.
boundaryWatch.note: United States at its applied 17.5 Critical score. The June 9 Fort Bliss GAO report (three deaths including destroyed-evidence homicide, $18M wasted) and the June 4 death-reporting rescission together deepen the ACC 1.4/INT 1.5 floor without altering the magnitude — same-regime continuation at the applied score.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Documented deliberate state obstruction of humanitarian access, a state-attributable mass-casualty event, or IPC Phase 5 famine-area confirmation converts the carry-forward to a scorable downgrade.
boundaryWatch.note: Nigeria at 1.9 points above Critical. West and Central Africa lean season active through August with 55 million food-insecure across the region; 34.7 million lean-season food-insecure and 3.5 million IDPs in Nigeria reflect non-state armed group and structural-fragility drivers priced at the 21.9 baseline.

emergingRisks.risk: The UAE continues to accumulate documented complicity evidence in Sudan's RSF genocide across five independent source chains; the June 9 Colombian-mercenary pipeline documentation (CIG 50-device tracking; Desert Wolves; Ghayathi facility) is now the fifth chain since February 2026.
emergingRisks.detail: The open June 8 proposal (23.4 → 18.4, Developing → Critical) rests on four source chains; the June 9 evidence adds a fifth. The ICJ case (filed March 2025) is the next adjudication milestone. Each additional cycle without RSF arms-transfer cessation deepens the record toward removing the unadjudicated magnitude cap. Verified direct UAE-force participation in Sudan would move toward a floor assessment.

emergingRisks.risk: Oracle's WARN Act sign-or-forfeit window closes June 15, six days from this cycle — the most proximate hard scoring trigger across all assessed entities; Oracle sits 0.6 points above Critical with the Washington class action and Missouri state investigation both active.
emergingRisks.detail: Approximately 30,000 workers remain in Oracle's sign-or-forfeit window. The zero-net-payout structure for newer and excluded workers compounds the EMP dimension each cycle the window remains open. Adverse adjudication on the remote-reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory constitutes an immediate crossing trigger from 0.6 points above Critical.

emergingRisks.risk: The Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak in DRC and Uganda continues to accelerate into the peak window; no approved vaccine exists for the Bundibugyo strain and contact-tracing coverage remains at approximately 20% of known transmission chains.
emergingRisks.detail: DRC at near-floor 2.3 and Uganda both rest on the absence of documented deliberate state obstruction of the WHO response. The 17-health-zone spread across Ituri and confirmed cross-border spread to Uganda as of June 6 represent a qualitative escalation in outbreak risk. The peak window runs through July 31 — the next structural review date.

emergingRisks.risk: Three Fortune 500 health insurers — Oracle, 3M, and Cigna — each sit within 0.3–0.6 points of the Developing/Critical boundary simultaneously, representing the highest concentration of proximate corporate-index boundary risk in the current cycle sequence.
emergingRisks.detail: Oracle (0.6pt), 3M (0.3pt), and Cigna (0.3pt) form a cluster of the narrowest active boundary margins in the corporate index. Oracle's trigger is date-certain (June 15); 3M's is July 31; Cigna's turns on adjudication timing in the AZ AG cartel suit or a separate parity ruling. If all three cross, it would be the largest single-cycle corporate-index band-crossing event since the pipeline launched.

emergingRisks.risk: EU AI Act GPAI transparency and systematic-risk documentation obligations take full effect August 2 — 54 days from this cycle — with no assessed AI lab having published compliance documentation.
emergingRisks.detail: Non-compliance fines reach 35 million euros or 7% of global annual turnover. The Great American AI Act (June 4–5, bipartisan draft) adds mandatory twice-yearly audits and $1M/day penalties. Both instruments bear on the same compliance-documentation gap. The August 2 EU AI Act date is the nearer of the two.

emergingRisks.risk: India's Bengali-Muslim mass-expulsion pattern accumulates further documentation each cycle; EU Returns Regulation enters force June 12, potentially activating a new refoulement-accountability signal for countries receiving deportees without individual rights assessments.
emergingRisks.detail: India at 15.6 (applied June 1) has accumulated three cycles of same-regime-continuation evidence on the deportation pattern. The EU Returns Regulation entering force June 12 creates a new accountability channel for the non-refoulement dimension if documented EU-facilitated returns occur without individual protection assessments. Confirmed state-attributable enforced disappearances among deportees at scale would constitute a new harm category independently scorable beyond the priced 15.6 baseline.

emergingRisks.risk: Haiti enters hurricane season at maximum structural vulnerability — 1.5 million IDPs, gang control of approximately 85% of Port-au-Prince, and an international security mission below operational strength — creating compounding risk for a state-failure-in-disaster-response scoring event.
emergingRisks.detail: Haiti's near-floor 4.7 encodes gang-driven collapse as the primary harm driver. Hurricane season runs June through November with Haiti at the intersection of maximum displacement concentration and minimum state-response capacity. A Category 1 or above landfall in Port-au-Prince with documented state failure to respond constitutes an independent scoring trigger above and beyond the priced baseline.

emergingRisks.risk: North Korean forced labor in EU supply chains is now documented across multiple sectors; entities with opaque tier-2 and tier-3 supplier relationships in the affected sectors face potential EMP and ACC scoring exposure when supply-chain audit obligations under the EU Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive activate.
emergingRisks.detail: The scanner surfaced North Korean forced-labor documentation across EU manufacturing supply chains as a sector alert this cycle. Fortune 500 entities with deep EU manufacturing exposure and opaque lower-tier supplier relationships are monitoring targets. No individual entity crossed a proposal threshold this cycle; the risk is tracked as an index-wide supply-chain transparency drift signal.

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — Floor and Near-Floor Cohort: Reinforcement Cycle
sectorAlerts.headline: Israel, Iran, DRC, Ethiopia, and Haiti confirm near-floor or floor positions with June 9 evidence deepening each pattern; no floor exits or new floor designations this cycle
observations: Israel at 0.0: Gaza seizure order covering 70% of territory, 40 military outposts (8 post-ceasefire), 119 killed in June, aid down 37%, 1.8M displaced — all reinforce active-perpetration at the absolute floor; cannot move lower
Iran at 2.5: 784 executions YTD, five political prisoners facing imminent execution, 127th consecutive week of prison protests; single-dominant-extremity hold maintained at near-floor

DRC at 2.3: Ebola PHEIC 515/91, Uganda cross-border spread confirmed June 6, no approved Bundibugyo vaccine, 20% contact-tracing coverage; exogenous-overlay hold intact absent deliberate state obstruction

Ethiopia at 4.7: June 1 non-democratic election (94.2% ruling-party airtime), press crackdown, EHPA suspension confirm repression-escalation-within-pattern; no new un-priced mass-atrocity category meeting the 0.0 threshold
Haiti at 4.7: Record 1.5M IDPs, 110k forced returns, gang-driven collapse, hurricane season opening; non-state-driven continuation already priced near floor

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — UAE/Sudan RSF Accountability: Duplicate-Suppression Cycle
sectorAlerts.headline: June 9 Colombian-mercenary pipeline documentation deepens the open UAE band-crossing proposal's evidentiary base to five independent source chains; no new proposal written per duplicate-suppression discipline
observations: Conflict Insights Group 50-device phone tracking (April 2025 – January 2026) documents UAE coordination with Colombian mercenaries for the Ghayathi/Abu Dhabi training facility and Desert Wolves brigade (drone pilots and artillery operators) deployed to Sudan

The June 9 CIG evidence is the same active-complicity-by-proxy pattern as the open June 8 proposal: it reinforces magnitude without materially changing the characterization — external complicity, ICJ unadjudicated, no documented in-territory UAE perpetration

The open June 8 proposal (23.4 → 18.4, Developing → Critical, −5.0, medium-high confidence) now rests on five independent source chains: UN FFM (Feb 2026), CIG (June 2026 and now extended June 9), Amnesty International, Sudan ICJ filing (March 2025), and Refugees International (June 8)
Sudan's 0.0 floor carries forward as in-territory active-perpetration; the external-sponsorship dimension is scored under UAE per no-double-count discipline

India at 15.6 (applied June 1): ~5,000 Bengali-Muslim deportations without due process deepen the EQU 1.3/EMP 1.4/ACC 1.4 profile set days earlier; EU Returns Regulation entering force June 12 is the next forward trigger

All three entities confirm the priced-continuation discipline: same-regime-continuation evidence on a recently-applied score does not trigger re-litigation unless a new un-priced harm category emerges

UnitedHealth at 10.2: coordinated multi-state AG investigation plus CEO resignation resolved as FILED-BUT-UNADJUDICATED — enforcement density rises (four concurrent sovereign actions) without crossing to scored; merits adjudication or settlement remains the only conversion trigger

sectorAlerts.sector: Ebola PHEIC — DRC and Uganda: Cross-Border Spread Confirmed
sectorAlerts.headline: Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak crosses into Uganda; DRC at 515 cases / 91 deaths with no approved vaccine and 20% contact-tracing coverage
observations: WHO PHEIC remains active; Bundibugyo strain has no approved vaccine — the standard ring-vaccination protocol used for Zaire strain is not applicable, limiting containment options
Uganda cross-border spread confirmed June 6 (CDC update); 17 of 36 Ituri health zones now affected in DRC; outbreak dynamics consistent with an accelerating trajectory through the peak window

DRC near-floor 2.3 and Uganda's boundary proximity both rest on the natural-disaster-overlay-non-double-count ruling: exogenous epidemiological shock on priced incapacity, absent documented deliberate state obstruction of WHO access

The July 31 peak window is the structural review deadline; documented state obstruction of WHO response (access denial, fund diversion, case-data suppression) is the conversion trigger from the current hold

sectorAlerts.sector: AI Labs — Regulatory Environment: Great American AI Act and EU AI Act Countdown
sectorAlerts.headline: Great American AI Act bipartisan draft circulates; EU AI Act GPAI obligations 54 days out; Anthropic's DC Circuit appeal remains the near-term lab-sector scoring pivot
observations: Great American AI Act (June 4–5 bipartisan draft): mandatory safety-framework publication, twice-yearly independent audits, $1M/day penalties, three-year state-preemption clause — at procedural/legislative stage; no assessed lab has published compliance documentation
EU AI Act GPAI obligations effective August 2: 54 days from this cycle; fines reach 35 million euros or 7% of global annual turnover; no assessed lab has published required transparency documentation

Anthropic's DC Circuit appeal (Anthropic v. Hegseth on Pentagon supply-chain-risk) has judges divided; favorable ruling triggers Functional/Established crossing from 59.1; adverse ruling produces downward movement — the near-term lab-sector scoring pivot
ASL-3 activation and METR agent-misalignment pilot cited as prospective safety positives for Anthropic; net-neutral composite confirmed at 59.1 absent adjudication or enactment

sectorAlerts.headline: 3M Australia $1.43B PFAS suit at Phase-2 MDL threshold July 31; Wisconsin $10M settlement attributed to Tyco/Johnson Controls — attribution boundary holds
observations: The Wisconsin $10M PFAS settlement is attributable to Tyco/Johnson Controls (the foam manufacturer), not 3M directly — over-attributing a third party's adjudicated reparation to 3M would misapply the methodology; attribution boundary re-confirmed this cycle
3M's own primary exposure is the Australia $1.43B PFAS claim at Phase-2 MDL (July 31); adverse adjudication attributable to 3M would cross from 0.3 points above Critical into Critical

The PFAS liability wave is a multi-entity sector alert: 3M, Chemours, DuPont, and their successor entities face overlapping federal and state actions; the methodology's attribution-boundary rule requires entity-specific adjudication before scoring

methodologyNotes.name: DUPLICATE-SUPPRESSION / SAME-PATTERN-REINFORCEMENT (United Arab Emirates — first application)
methodologyNotes.description: When new evidence is assessed and confirmed as the same evidence base underpinning an open change proposal — deepening documentation without materially changing the characterization or magnitude — no duplicate proposal is generated. The open proposal is reinforced and left unmodified. The June 9 UAE-Colombian-mercenary pipeline evidence (CIG 50-device phone tracking; Ghayathi/Abu Dhabi training facility; Desert Wolves) is the same active-complicity-by-proxy pattern as the open June 8 proposal (23.4 → 18.4, Developing → Critical, −5.0): it strengthens the evidentiary chain without changing the external-complicity characterization, the ICJ-unadjudicated magnitude cap, or the absence of documented in-territory UAE perpetration. Per duplicate-suppression discipline, no second proposal written; the pending June 8 proposal is the operative record.

methodologyNotes.name: COORDINATED-INVESTIGATION-STILL-PRE-ADJUDICATION (UnitedHealth Group — scorability question resolved NO)
methodologyNotes.description: A coordinated multi-sovereign investigation raises enforcement density but does not constitute an adjudication. Under the established FILED-BUT-UNADJUDICATED rule, the number of coordinating jurisdictions and the prominence of the investigation do not convert it to scored harm; only a merits determination, settlement, or formal charge does. The June 9 assessment of UnitedHealth's coordinated multi-state AG investigation (plus DOJ MA-fraud criminal probe and CEO resignation) was resolved on this basis: enforcement density is NOT adjudication. CEO resignation is governance churn, not a compassion-harm signal. Confirmed at 10.2; conversion trigger is merits adjudication or settlement.

methodologyNotes.name: REPRESSION-ESCALATION-WITHIN-PATTERN (Ethiopia — scorability question resolved NO)
methodologyNotes.description: When a repressive one-party state's published score already encodes the systemic repression profile (ACC/EQU/SYS/INT at or near 1.0), additional evidence of electoral manipulation, press crackdown, and civil-society suppression represents escalation within the priced pattern rather than a new un-priced harm category. The conversion trigger toward the 0.0 active-perpetration floor requires documented mass-casualty atrocity in a new theater or systematic elimination of a defined civilian population group — not incremental deepening of a priced repression profile. Ethiopia's June 1 election, press crackdown, and EHPA suspension were resolved on this basis: repression-escalation-within-pattern, near-floor hold at 4.7.

methodologyNotes.name: THIRD-PARTY-REPARATION-ATTRIBUTION-BOUNDARY (3M — Wisconsin settlement re-confirmed)
methodologyNotes.description: An adjudicated reparation payment by a third party — even within the same supply chain, product category, or litigation cluster — is not attributable to a separately-assessed entity without independent adjudication of that entity's liability. The Wisconsin $10M PFAS settlement is an adjudicated reparation by Tyco/Johnson Controls (the foam manufacturer and seller), not by 3M. Attributing it to 3M would over-score; it is sub-threshold and boundary for 3M, whose own primary exposure (Australia $1.43B PFAS Phase-2 MDL) remains pre-adjudication. Attribution boundary re-confirmed at the 20.3 hold.

methodologyNotes.name: ACTIVE-PERPETRATION-FLOOR-REINFORCEMENT (Israel — re-affirmed June 9)
methodologyNotes.description: The Gaza seizure order (70% of Gaza, 40 military outposts, 8 post-ceasefire), 119 Palestinians killed in June, aid down 37%, and 1.8M displaced reinforce the active-perpetration floor at 0.0. The floor is fully constituted at all-dimension 1.0 with zero integration premium; it cannot move lower. The Microsoft Azure/Unit 8200 compelled remediation is scored under Microsoft, never against this sovereign floor — no double-counting of the same conflict's accountability record.

methodologyNotes.name: PRICED / SAME-REGIME-CONTINUATION (United States, Bolivia, India — re-confirmed)
methodologyNotes.description: All three entities are at recently applied lower scores (US 17.5 from 23.4 applied June 9; Bolivia 18.4 from 28.4 applied June 9; India 15.6 from 22.7 applied June 1) and the June 9 evidence is same-regime continuation already priced. US: Fort Bliss GAO report reinforces the ACC 1.4/INT 1.5 floor without altering the magnitude. Bolivia: June 8 impunity law and 10 killed/365+ arrested are the exact events the applied downgrade encoded. India: Bengali-Muslim deportations deepen the EQU 1.3/EMP 1.4/ACC 1.4 profile set days earlier. Confirmed; no re-litigation.

methodologyNotes.name: COMPELLED-REMEDY-NOT-SELF-CORRECTION (Microsoft — positive-watch continuation)
methodologyNotes.description: The HR Review Board is now confirmed created — formation has moved from announcement to existence. Under COMPELLED-REMEDY-NOT-SELF-CORRECTION, this remains AB2 anchor 3 (course correction under pressure), not anchor 4–5 self-initiation. The board was compelled by the August 2025 Guardian/+972/Local Call exposé; the Covington review scope was limited by design; the board is a prospective governance commitment with no observable outputs. No verified operative effect — no contract blocked or exited — has been documented since June 7. Maximum defensible positive move remains +1.3 (to 66.6), sub-threshold. Conversion trigger: verified HR Review Board decision blocking or exiting a harmful national-security contract.

methodologyNotes.name: ROTATION-CONFIRM (Walmart, Cigna, Boeing, Target, New York City)
methodologyNotes.description: Five rotation entities confirmed at published scores without acute negative events. Cigna (20.3) flagged as a boundary case at 0.3 points above Critical (claim-denial and algorithmic-review profile; AZ AG cartel suit is the active adjudication channel). Target disambiguated as Target Corporation (Retail, Fortune 500 rank 69, benchmark rank 1) — correct entity at 92.8 Exemplary. New York City published 56.3 vs canonical reconstruct 56.2 (diff 0.1, within 0.5 tolerance — not a math-hygiene flag). Boeing and Walmart both confirmed stable with no acute event in the lookback window.

forwardTriggers.trigger: EU Returns Regulation enters force. Documented EU-facilitated return without individual assessment where a third-country national faces persecution constitutes a scored signal. India at 15.6 is the primary non-refoulement watch entity; confirmed state-attributable enforced disappearances at scale would constitute a new harm category independently scorable beyond the priced baseline.

WARN Act last-working-day sign-or-forfeit window closes for approximately 30,000 affected workers. Any adjudicated or corroborated finding on the remote-reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory in the Washington state class action or Missouri state investigation constitutes an immediate scoring trigger from 0.6 points above Critical.
Australia $1.43B PFAS Phase-2 MDL deadline. Adverse adjudication or settlement of the Australia PFAS claim attributable to 3M directly would cross from 0.3 points above Critical into Critical.

Ebola Bundibugyo peak window through July with 17-health-zone spread, Uganda cross-border confirmed, and no approved vaccine. Documented deliberate state obstruction of the WHO response breaks the NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY hold at the near-floor 2.3 composite.

EU AI Act GPAI transparency and systematic-risk documentation obligations take full effect. Fines up to 35 million euros or 7% of global annual turnover. No assessed AI lab has published compliance documentation — 54 days from this cycle.

ICJ ruling in Sudan v. UAE (filed March 2025 alleging UAE complicity in genocide). Verified cessation of RSF arms transfers and mercenary supply is the primary reversal signal for the proposed 18.4 Critical composite. Documented direct UAE-force participation in Sudan would move toward a floor assessment. The open June 8 proposal is reinforced by five independent source chains as of June 9.

Judicial annulment or legislative repeal of the June 8 presumption-of-legality military-force law is the primary reversal signal. Further military deployments with documented casualties under the signed law reinforce the applied 18.4 Critical baseline.

Appellate review of Murphy (D. Mass.) and Cobb (D.D.C.) deportation injunctions. Reinstatement of the rescinded June 4 ICE post-release death-reporting requirement is the first upward-direction signal within the applied 17.5 Critical baseline.

Verified Human Rights Review Board decision demonstrably blocking or exiting a harmful national-security contract — conversion trigger from positive-watch to scorable upgrade. Quiet reinstatement of terminated Unit 8200 access is a negative trigger.

Merits adjudication or settlement of any of the four converging sovereign enforcement actions — DOJ MA-billing criminal probe, AZ AG eight-insurer cartel suit, Lifepoint federal complaint, or DOJ Antitrust MultiPlan grand-jury subpoena. AZ AG adjudication would simultaneously reach seven other Fortune 500 health insurers.

DC Circuit ruling on Anthropic v. Hegseth. Favorable ruling triggers Functional/Established boundary crossing from 59.1; adverse ruling produces downward movement. Enacted Great American AI Act compliance documentation would be independently scorable.

Adjudicated claim-denial finding or mental-health parity ruling would push into Critical from 0.3 points above the boundary. AZ AG eight-insurer cartel suit adjudication is the sector-wide channel reaching Cigna alongside seven other Fortune 500 health insurers.

Verified executed governance reform on military-cloud human-rights review that produces observable outcomes — a documented decision blocking or exiting a harmful contract — constitutes a scorable upward signal from the exact 40/41 band boundary. New entity-specific adverse evidence on Project Nimbus is an immediate downward trigger.

dailyOpeningQuestion.text: If the AZ AG eight-insurer cartel suit reaches a merits adjudication finding, does the claim-denial coordination pattern it alleges constitute simultaneous ACC dimension harm across all eight named entities — or does the methodology require separate adjudication of each insurer's individual conduct to score any of them?
dailyOpeningQuestion.tensionFraming: UnitedHealth, Cigna, and six other Fortune 500 health insurers named in the same cartel action — methodology must resolve whether shared adjudication constitutes shared scored harm.
